title: Summer Catalog

jokes.text: Swiss family ron-binson- that hat is dope.
jokes.setup: Tom sees Ron wearing what appears to be a coonskin/Davy Crockett hat
jokes.punchline: Makes a pun combining 'Swiss Family Robinson' with Ron's name
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits Tom's trendy character. Good economy and character-specific humor.

jokes.text: You mind if I rock that bad larry on my dome? Wear it, on my head? Try it on the ladies?
jokes.setup: Tom wants to try on Ron's hat
jokes.punchline: Uses unnecessarily elaborate slang ('bad larry on my dome') then immediately clarifies with plain English
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character moment - his tendency to use elaborate slang then clarify it defeats the purpose of slang entirely.

jokes.text: Hi. I'm tom. I have a raccoon on my head.
jokes.setup: Tom is trying to use Ron's hat as a pickup line prop
jokes.punchline: Delivers the most straightforward, unromantic description possible
jokes.explanation: Great economy - the complete failure of Tom's pickup strategy in one perfectly blunt line.

jokes.text: Excuse me, my friend over here was digging through your trash, And I think we may have a lot in common.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to use the raccoon hat as a conversation starter
jokes.punchline: Suggests the woman has something in common with a raccoon because it digs through trash
jokes.explanation: Absurdist logic that's insulting in the most oblivious way possible. Classic Tom.

jokes.text: Because you've been running around my hat all da-- Head all day.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to make a pickup line about the raccoon
jokes.punchline: Messes up his own terrible pun mid-delivery
jokes.explanation: The cringe comes from Tom failing at his own bad pickup line. Character-specific incompetence.

jokes.text: Nice hat. Want to bone?
jokes.setup: Tom's elaborate pickup attempts have all failed
jokes.punchline: Abandons all subtlety for the most direct possible approach
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and escalation. The bluntness after elaborate failures is what makes it funny.

jokes.text: yes, I am a hunter, and it's you season
jokes.setup: Tom making another hunting-themed pickup line to go with the hat
jokes.punchline: Awkward twist on 'duck season/rabbit season' that makes no logical sense
jokes.explanation: Weak pun structure but fits Tom's character of trying too hard with wordplay.

jokes.text: Animal on the head, Manimal in the bed.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining his pickup strategy philosophy
jokes.punchline: Creates a rhyming motto that references the 80s TV show 'Manimal'
jokes.explanation: The 'Manimal' reference is perfectly absurd and character-appropriate for Tom's pop culture obsession.

jokes.text: Damn, girl, your hotness killed my raccoon.
jokes.setup: Tom's final attempt at a pickup line
jokes.punchline: Blames the woman's attractiveness for the death of his hat
jokes.explanation: Highly original absurdist logic. The line is completely nonsensical but delivered with confidence.

jokes.text: I have a raccoon hat. I'm an interesting person.
jokes.setup: Tom's confidence despite complete failure
jokes.punchline: Equates owning a raccoon hat with being interesting
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Tom's delusional self-confidence. Great economy and character insight.

jokes.text: Wow, I love that hat.
jokes.setup: Tom has completely failed with every woman using the hat
jokes.punchline: A woman finally likes the hat just as Tom is giving up
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom irony - success comes just after giving up. Well-structured but predictable.

jokes.text: When I saw my friends hiding through the window, I drove to a gas station, called the cops, And told them people had broken into my home.
jokes.setup: Leslie wants to throw Ron a surprise, Ron explains why he hates surprises
jokes.punchline: Ron's extreme reaction to a surprise party involved calling police on his own friends
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Ron's paranoid, anti-social nature. The extreme overreaction is both absurd and completely believable for the character.

jokes.text: Think of the September issue of vogue, But it's more important to pawnee, mainly because we don't get vogue here.
jokes.setup: Leslie comparing the importance of the parks catalog
jokes.punchline: Undermines her own grandiose comparison by admitting they don't get Vogue
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Leslie's tendency to build things up then accidentally deflate her own hype.

jokes.text: Centerfold? Always the best part. Am I right, justin?
jokes.setup: Tom making a comment about centerfolds in what's supposed to be a parks catalog
jokes.punchline: Appeals to Justin who apparently isn't there or doesn't respond
jokes.explanation: Tom's inappropriate comment and failed attempt at male bonding. Mildly cringey.

jokes.text: Damn. You broke up six weeks ago.
jokes.setup: Tom's failed attempt to get backup on his centerfold comment
jokes.punchline: April matter-of-factly explains why Justin didn't respond
jokes.explanation: April's deadpan delivery explaining Tom's obliviousness. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Isn't that that creepy guy? Morgan, the pedophile?
jokes.setup: Looking through Jerry's softball photos for the catalog
jokes.punchline: Casually identifies someone as a pedophile in the photos
jokes.explanation: Dark humor but not particularly clever. Relies on shock value more than craft.

jokes.text: Come on, I didn't know that. He seemed okay. We had dinner together.
jokes.setup: Jerry defending his choice to include the pedophile in photos
jokes.punchline: Reveals he had dinner with him, making it worse
jokes.explanation: Jerry's innocent obliviousness making a bad situation worse. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Can you photoshop your life with better decisions, jerry?
jokes.setup: Jerry offers to photoshop the pedophile's face out
jokes.punchline: April suggests Jerry photoshop his entire life instead
jokes.explanation: Clever play on Photoshop concept applied to life decisions. Perfect April voice - creative cruelty.

jokes.text: Modern life. Where are we running? Sometimes what we want is not always where we are.
jokes.setup: April presenting her concept for the parks catalog cover
jokes.punchline: Pretentious pseudo-philosophical gibberish presented seriously
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of pretentious art-speak. Very April to present meaningless profundity with complete seriousness.

jokes.text: Are we alone? Is the real winter inside our hearts? We're all struggling for definition In a world that resists our inquiries.
jokes.setup: Continuation of April's pretentious presentation
jokes.punchline: More meaningless philosophical questions that sound deep but say nothing
jokes.explanation: Continues the art-speak parody but loses some impact through repetition. Still character-appropriate.

jokes.text: that child looks like it's abandoned, So basically, boo.
jokes.setup: Leslie rejecting April's concept photo for multiple reasons
jokes.punchline: Sums up her rejection with 'basically, boo' after listing serious problems
jokes.explanation: Leslie's cheerful dismissal with 'boo' after serious criticism. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: That's a personal photo. That's-- Shouldn't be-- it must've...
jokes.setup: A personal photo of Tom accidentally appears in the presentation
jokes.punchline: Tom's flustered reaction to his private photo being displayed
jokes.explanation: Standard embarrassing photo gag. Visual humor we can't fully see, but Tom's reaction sells it.

jokes.text: I think that could actually work as our cover photo. I'm cool with it if you guys are.
jokes.setup: Tom's embarrassment over his personal photo being shown
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivots to suggesting they use his embarrassing personal photo as the cover
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character moment - turns embarrassment into narcissistic opportunity. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: why is my cup so tiny?
jokes.setup: Andy looking at something in the photo
jokes.punchline: Andy's innocent confusion about what's presumably a normal-sized cup
jokes.explanation: Pure Andy - innocent and confused by normal things. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Happy six-month anniversary. I got you a gift. Uh, it's a chicken-salad sandwich.
jokes.setup: Ann celebrating a relationship milestone
jokes.punchline: Her 'gift' is just a regular sandwich she probably brought for lunch
jokes.explanation: Ann's practical, unromantic approach to relationships. The anticlimax is perfectly character-driven.

jokes.text: Swiss army watch, mofo.
jokes.setup: Ann gives a simple sandwich as an anniversary gift
jokes.punchline: Mark produces an actual thoughtful gift and celebrates with unexpected slang
jokes.explanation: Good contrast to Ann's underwhelming gift. 'Mofo' is unexpected from Mark, adding character surprise.

jokes.text: Oh, don't worry about it. He's dead. Oh, my god. Oh, no, I'm just kidding. He's sleeping.
jokes.setup: Mark wondering if they can kiss in a hospital room
jokes.punchline: Ann jokes about the patient being dead, then immediately clarifies
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with dark humor. Well-structured but not particularly original.

jokes.text: That guy really seemed dead.
jokes.setup: Previous joke about the sleeping patient looking dead
jokes.punchline: Mark's matter-of-fact assessment that reinforces the visual
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that reinforces the visual gag. Good economy but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: that's, like, the boy version of softball.
jokes.setup: Andy mentions he played baseball in high school after April mentions softball
jokes.punchline: April dismissively describes baseball as the 'boy version of softball'
jokes.explanation: Clever gender role reversal that subverts expectations. Perfect April voice - dismissive and contrary.

jokes.text: April threw the smoked salmon on the roof.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking if they have everything for the picnic
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact revelation that April threw food on the roof
jokes.explanation: Perfectly random April behavior delivered matter-of-factly by Andy. Great character integration and absurdist humor.

jokes.text: I'm sorry.
jokes.setup: April threw smoked salmon on the roof
jokes.punchline: Completely flat, insincere apology
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan delivery. The economy is exceptional - maximum character in minimum words.

jokes.text: Maybe in your heart, you knew it was from me, But you came anyway.
jokes.setup: Mark questions why he believed the fake text
jokes.punchline: Leslie's romantic interpretation of her own deception
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie optimism and romantic delusion. She turns her own manipulation into a love story.

jokes.text: There's no such thing as a city-planning emergency.
jokes.setup: Tom has been texting Mark about a city-planning emergency
jokes.punchline: Mark's logical observation that such emergencies don't exist
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Tom's dramatics. Mark's practical voice comes through clearly.

jokes.text: Ann-danawicz... Or merkins.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to create a couple name for Ann and Mark
jokes.punchline: Accidentally creates 'merkins' which is slang for pubic wigs
jokes.explanation: Unintentional innuendo from Tom's name-combining attempt. The accidental humor fits his character.

jokes.text: We don't have any pictures Of just the two of us. Plus, I've always said We should go on more dates with tom.
jokes.setup: Mark agreeing to the photo shoot
jokes.punchline: Ann sarcastically claiming she wanted to go on dates with Tom
jokes.explanation: Ann's dry sarcasm about Tom's intrusion. Good character voice and relationship dynamic.

jokes.text: And would it kill you to maybe put on some lipstick? Do you even own lipstick?
jokes.setup: Tom directing the photo shoot
jokes.punchline: Insults Ann's appearance while asking for a favor
jokes.explanation: Tom's tactless direction fits his character. Cringey but believable inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: Sweater swap. She noticed. You were right.
jokes.setup: Two old men appear to have switched sweaters
jokes.punchline: Andy's casual observation about their clothing swap
jokes.explanation: Visual gag we can't see but Andy's matter-of-fact delivery sells it. Good economy.

jokes.text: I thought you were dead, clarence. No, I'm gonna outlive you, then I'm gonna nail your wife.
jokes.setup: David casually mentioning he thought Clarence was dead
jokes.punchline: Clarence's aggressive sexual boast in response
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation between old men. The unexpected aggression from elderly characters is amusing.

jokes.text: When I say 'parks,' you say 'department.' Parks. Apartment!
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to get the old directors to do a cheer
jokes.punchline: Clarence completely messes up the simple response
jokes.explanation: Classic comedy of Leslie's optimism meeting reality. Simple but effective failure humor.

jokes.text: I'm starving. I only had one breakfast.
jokes.setup: Ron complaining about being hungry
jokes.punchline: Implies he normally has multiple breakfasts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson line - reveals his massive appetite in character-specific way. Excellent economy and character integration.

jokes.text: I could smell it in your purse Before I even parked my car. And now it's gone, And I hate everything.
jokes.setup: Ron ate bacon Leslie brought as a trail snack
jokes.punchline: His superhuman bacon-detection abilities and dramatic despair
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Ron's bacon obsession and dramatic tendencies. Great escalation from detection to despair.

jokes.text: Keep slithering, keep slithering!
jokes.setup: Tom directing the photo shoot
jokes.punchline: Gives bizarre direction for Ann and Mark to 'slither'
jokes.explanation: Tom's pretentious photo direction. The repetition and weird choice of 'slithering' is appropriately cringey.

jokes.text: Do what mark's doing. He's doing great. I'm not doing anything, so...
jokes.setup: Tom praising Mark's modeling
jokes.punchline: Mark flatly points out he's not doing anything
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Tom's delusion and Mark's practical honesty. Solid structure and economy.

jokes.text: Women, uh, need a lot of blood To--to flow through to- -to the baby cents, Which leaves less to the brain, you see.
jokes.setup: Leslie mentions wanting to be the first female parks director
jokes.punchline: Clarence's incredibly sexist pseudo-scientific explanation
jokes.explanation: Cringey sexism from elderly character. Makes up for weak craft through character authenticity and cringe factor.

jokes.text: I'd stay away from leadership roles, Uh, for your own safety.
jokes.setup: Clarence's sexist explanation about women's blood flow
jokes.punchline: Frames his sexism as concern for Leslie's wellbeing
jokes.explanation: Continues the cringe from Clarence's sexism. The fake concern adds another layer of awfulness.

jokes.text: How about 'life is a picnic...With you'? Whoa. Then it could be about a girl... Or peyton manning.
jokes.setup: Andy writing a song about a picnic
jokes.punchline: Suggests the love song could be about either a girl or Peyton Manning
jokes.explanation: Perfectly random Andy logic. The casual inclusion of Peyton Manning as a romantic option is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: Clarence was old and weak, So I put in for a transfer, swooped, And pushed him out.
jokes.setup: David explaining how he got the parks director job
jokes.punchline: Openly admits to workplace predatory behavior
jokes.explanation: David's casual admission of workplace ruthlessness. Good character moment showing his lack of shame.

jokes.text: who gives a crap about this crap?
jokes.setup: David explaining his motivations for the parks job
jokes.punchline: Openly states he doesn't care about parks work
jokes.explanation: David's blunt dismissal of parks work. Great economy and character honesty, if unpleasant.

jokes.text: Ann, you look miserable! Terrible, terrible!
jokes.setup: Tom directing the photo shoot
jokes.punchline: Gives the worst possible direction by telling Ann she looks terrible
jokes.explanation: Tom's completely counterproductive photography direction. Cringey but character-appropriate incompetence.

jokes.text: Maxim or good housekeeping? I'm not sure which one is the insult.
jokes.setup: Tom making magazine comparisons about Ann's look
jokes.punchline: Ann points out that both comparisons are insulting
jokes.explanation: Good comeback from Ann showing Tom's tactlessness. Both magazine references are indeed insulting in different ways.

jokes.text: How could someone so hot be so bad at looking hot?
jokes.setup: Tom frustrated with Ann's photo shoot performance
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that insults while complimenting
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom inappropriate comment. The logic is both complimentary and insulting simultaneously.

jokes.text: we may have to go nude.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to solve the photo shoot problems
jokes.punchline: Suggests the most inappropriate solution possible
jokes.explanation: Predictable escalation to inappropriate suggestion. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: If you're menstruating, you'll attract bears.
jokes.setup: Clarence telling Leslie she shouldn't lead them to the picnic
jokes.punchline: Cites completely false information about bears and menstruation
jokes.explanation: References the old Anchorman joke but fits Clarence's sexist character. Recycled humor but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I just whizzed on a butterfly.
jokes.setup: The group arrives at their picnic destination
jokes.punchline: Clarence announces his inappropriate bathroom behavior
jokes.explanation: Absurd and gross announcement that fits the character's complete lack of social awareness.

jokes.text: He smoked pot in the office And in all the parks constantly. It was the early '90s, but also it's ridiculous That marijuana is illegal.
jokes.setup: Ron revealing Michael was on parole
jokes.punchline: Michael admits to constant drug use and goes on a legalization rant
jokes.explanation: Michael's character as the stoner parks director. Good escalation from admission to political rant.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. I can't hear hippies.
jokes.setup: Michael asking Ron about marijuana legalization
jokes.punchline: Ron's flat dismissal of hippies entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson line - dismissive, economical, and perfectly character-consistent. Excellent delivery and memorability.

jokes.text: The super old one. I'm an eyebrow girl. I want to make out with him and chew his eyebrows off.
jokes.setup: April asked which old man she'd sleep with
jokes.punchline: Gives disturbing detailed preference for eyebrow consumption
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly disturbing April moment. The specificity of 'chew his eyebrows off' is memorably gross and character-perfect.

jokes.text: oh, I want water. I'm so old. I need to drink water.
jokes.setup: Someone asks for water
jokes.punchline: April mocks elderly people's basic needs
jokes.explanation: April's cruel but character-appropriate mocking. Not particularly clever but fits her personality.

jokes.text: Yes, exactly like that, In that I wanted it, and then I took it.
jokes.setup: Someone comparing David's job acquisition to claiming food
jokes.punchline: David proudly confirms his ruthless approach
jokes.explanation: David's unashamed admission of workplace predatory behavior. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I've planted marijuana in community gardens All over the city, and it hasn't hurt anyone.
jokes.setup: Michael continuing his marijuana legalization argument
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to widespread illegal gardening
jokes.explanation: Michael's casual admission of city-wide drug cultivation. Good character moment showing his complete lack of boundaries.

jokes.text: can we do one funny-face one?
jokes.setup: After a tense, awful photo session with hostile old men
jokes.punchline: Clarence innocently asks for a silly photo as if they're having fun
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic contrast to the hostile atmosphere. Clarence's obliviousness to the tension is both sweet and absurd.

jokes.text: Their mouths are just so old.
jokes.setup: April trying to Photoshop the old men to look happy
jokes.punchline: Explains the difficulty through their ancient mouths
jokes.explanation: April's matter-of-fact observation about age. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: I call this one 'the future.' It's completely blurry.
jokes.setup: Tom showing his artistic photography
jokes.punchline: Names a blurry photo 'the future' as if it's profound
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of pretentious photography. Tom's art-speak for technical failure is very character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You mean the 'unhappy wife' photos?
jokes.setup: Ann asking about the normal swing photos
jokes.punchline: Tom categorizes them as 'unhappy wife' photos
jokes.explanation: Tom's tactless categorization of Ann's photos. Cringey but character-consistent insensitivity.

jokes.text: Hello, disgusting fragrance flap.
jokes.setup: Leslie replacing her welcome letter with a cologne ad
jokes.punchline: Describes the cologne sample as 'disgusting fragrance flap'
jokes.explanation: Leslie's frustrated but accurate description of cologne samples. Good observational humor.

jokes.text: Seriously? You're not even gonna show me a fake? Sometimes the confidence confuses people.
jokes.setup: Tom getting carded and not having ID
jokes.punchline: Explains he relies on confidence instead of fake ID
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom delusion - thinks his confidence substitutes for actual ID. Great character insight.

jokes.text: Are we gonna hate each other someday?
jokes.setup: Leslie realizing all the former directors hate each other
jokes.punchline: Innocent question about her and Ron's future relationship
jokes.explanation: Sweet Leslie moment showing her concern about friendship. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Of course, my first act as city manager Would be to double the size of the parks department, Although I do expect a fight from ron. But I'll win.
jokes.setup: Leslie's talking head about potentially becoming city manager
jokes.punchline: Mirrors Ron's earlier comment but with opposite goals, plus confident 'I'll win'
jokes.explanation: Great callback structure showing Leslie and Ron's opposing views. The confident 'I'll win' is perfectly Leslie.

jokes.text: People are idiots, leslie.
jokes.setup: Leslie wondering why anyone would eat non-breakfast food
jokes.punchline: Ron's matter-of-fact assessment of humanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron line - simple, direct, and completely consistent with his worldview. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: He made a bong out of a taxidermied raccoon? I caught him smoking pot out of it Not three feet from where we're standing.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Michael's water-pipe making skills
jokes.punchline: Ron reveals Michael made a bong from a stuffed raccoon at this very location
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and ties back to the episode's opening raccoon theme. Original and memorable image.

tmdb_overview: Preparing the Summer Events Catalog, Leslie decides to take Ron and the past directors of the Parks Department out for a picnic lunch. Tom enlists the help of Ann and Mark to pose for the cover photo.
insight: Summer catalog logistics anchor 70 jokes, but midseason slump shows in modest 72.1 index.
